ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'chicken nugget' class (FOODON_03315091); no specific 10-piece spicy meal variant exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers breaded fried chicken nuggets; no spicy meal-set variant exists in FoodEx2 hierarchy. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for spicy chicken nuggets meal; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 1 meal (10 pieces of chicken nuggets with sides)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 307 g, representing a 10-piece spicy chicken nuggets meal as sold at a fast food outlet in Singapore, inclusive of nuggets and typical accompaniments.

nutrient_source.title: 10Pc Chicken Nuggets Meal (D) Spicy — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for '10Pc Chicken Nuggets Meal (D) Spicy' (cr_id G00010016). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, dietary fibre, trans fat, and detailed fatty acid breakdown are not reported in the HPB entry; these fields are set to -1 or estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries for breaded fried chicken nuggets (USDA SR Legacy NDB 05062 / FDC ID 171477) and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of breaded fried chicken products — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entry for chicken nuggets / breaded chicken
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific spicy 10-piece nuggets meal. GI of 66 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for breaded fried chicken nuggets (GI ~66, medium category), which is the closest approved analogous food. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_g per serving / 100 = 66 × (14.3 g/100g × 307 g serving / 100) / 100 = 66 × 43.9 g / 100 = 29.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 708 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this product; sodium at 708 mg/100 g (equivalent to approximately 2,175 mg per 307 g serving) substantially exceeds the recommended daily sodium ceiling of 2,000 mg, and spicy seasoning may contribute additional undeclared sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Deep-fried ultra-processed product with refined wheat breading, medium GI (~66), high fat content, and estimated trans fat that impairs insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular consumption of this product; the refined breading raises postprandial blood glucose (GI ~66), the high fat content (22.6 g/100 g) slows gastric emptying unpredictably, and estimated trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) further worsens insulin resistance — portion control and infrequent consumption are strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (22.6 g/100 g), estimated saturated fat (5.8 g/100 g), trans fat (0.3 g/100 g), cholesterol (50 mg/100 g), and very high sodium (708 mg/100 g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid this product; the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, high sodium, and cholesterol is strongly atherogenic and hypertensive, and regular consumption of deep-fried ultra-processed chicken products is associated with increased risk of coronary heart disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); chilli is a nightshade ingredient that may exacerbate joint inflammation in RA-sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product; deep-fried preparation significantly increases AGE load, estimated trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) is strongly pro-inflammatory, and chilli (a nightshade) may worsen solanine-related joint inflammation in sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The 10-Piece Spicy Chicken Nuggets Meal is a fast food set meal sold in Singapore, consisting of ten bite-sized deep-fried breaded chicken nuggets seasoned with spicy chilli flavouring, typically served with a beverage and side accompaniments. The nuggets are made from processed chicken meat coated in a refined wheat flour batter, deep-fried in vegetable oil, and dusted or marinated with chilli-based spice blends. The texture is crispy on the outside with a moist, tender chicken interior, and the flavour profile is savoury, salty, and moderately to intensely spicy. This product is classified as ultra-processed and is halal-certified for the Singapore market. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 326 kcal, 16.5 g protein, 22.6 g fat (including estimated 5.8 g saturated fat and 0.3 g trans fat), and 14.3 g carbohydrates, with a very high sodium content of 708 mg — exceeding the 600 mg/100 g threshold that flags a food as unsuitable for hypertension management. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 66 (medium), driven by the refined wheat breading. Clinically, this product is of high concern for hypertension (very high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (medium GI, high fat, trans fat impairing insulin sensitivity), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and sodium). It is also of concern for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to AGE load from deep-frying and the presence of nightshade-derived chilli. This meal is commonly consumed at lunch or dinner and is a popular fast food option in Singapore's hawker and quick-service restaurant landscape.

Fast food chicken nuggets nutrition facts Singapore

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients and micronutrients are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010016, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and dietary fibre are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries for breaded fried chicken nuggets and documented in notes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Overall confidence is high given the exact HPB source match for core values.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (5.8 g/100g), monounsaturated fat (9.4 g/100g), polyunsaturated fat (5.6 g/100g), trans fat (0.3 g/100g), and dietary fibre (0.6 g/100g) are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this item. These values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Chicken nuggets, breaded, frozen, prepared' (FDC ID 171477) as the closest approved analogous source. These fields should be verified against the manufacturer's nutrition panel when available. 2. Vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12), folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and water_g are set to -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this food, and no approved secondary source provides confirmed values for this specific product. 3. Potassium (250 mg/100g), calcium (11 mg/100g), magnesium (22 mg/100g), iron (0.62 mg/100g), and cholesterol (50 mg/100g) are sourced directly from the HPB Foodo record. 4. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for breaded fried chicken nuggets; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. 5. Serving size of 307 g is estimated based on a typical 10-piece fast food nuggets meal portion; actual weight may vary by outlet. 6. The product is tagged halal per the input cultural dietary_tags field. 7. Trans fat value of 0.3 g/100g is an estimate from the USDA analogous source and should be confirmed against the product label. 8. Fields set to -1: vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g — detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific fast food product are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16.
